author: potuz
content: Nice! Lodestar and Prysm can handle missing Payloads, will see if we split on reorging payloads now

author: potuz
content: Ah no, will see for missed blocks now

author: nc1234
content: Is there a list of test cases somewhere?

author: potuz
content: I haven't worked any forkchoice tests yet

author: potuz
content: will be testing the usual culprits

author: jtraglia
content: Please share a list of what you test. I would be curious to see it.

author: potuz
content: Are you guys sending the right attestations for full with committee index=1 when there's a missed block <@586161934425128960> <@1035747431461167125> ?

author: potuz
content: We are immediately broken on a missed slot. Lodestar doesn't seem to be broken and follows Prysm, but Prysm reorgs any incoming block based on full. I suspect that  actually Prysm is doing the right thing and it's trying to build on full when attestations from the previous committee told it that it should be on empty!

author: nflaig
content: no, I don't think we do, same as for ptc, that's not implemented right now, I guess the attestation part is more important for initial testing

author: potuz
content: ok, I don't think we can really go to devnet with such a broken forkchoice, I think we are also voting on empty

author: potuz
content: but forkchoice will make empty be head

author: potuz
content: I think Prysm is doing the right thing here as a proposer, but the wrong here as an attester, and Lodestar is the same

author: potuz
content: although you guys seem to be acting wrong as proposers too

author: potuz
content: PTC can be ignored, but the attestation path should not

author: potuz
content: we have the PR to include this so I can test if Prysm correctly votes for the full block

author: nc1234
content: If I understand correctly, there is a full block at slot n, slot n + 1 has skipped slot, in slot n + 2 Lodestar is voting with committee index = 0. So Lodestar's head block is empty variant of slot n. Prysm is building on full variant of slot n, which is correct. But Prysm's attester duty is voting with committee index = 0. Am I understanding this correctly?

author: potuz
content: the problem is not n+2

author: potuz
content: the problem is n+1

author: potuz
content: in that slot Lodestar should be voting for (n, full)

author: potuz
content: during n+2 the proposer builds on top of (n, full) and lodestar should correctly vote for (n+2, index=0) which correctly does

author: nc1234
content: I see. Currently we are still doing Electra's attestation in epbs-devnet-0 branch which committee index is always 0. We will get this updated after we've merged our fork choice PR.

author: potuz
content: Yeah I'll post this in the main channel just in case

author: potuz
content: I confirm that even with 33% Lodestar, things break cause Lodestar attests for empty and tries to build on top of full, so we reorg Lodestar's block and that leads to mayhem

author: potuz
content: Forkchoice is going to be fun to test in devnets... <@199561711278227457> prepare yourself to dream about me in the following months
